ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general instant coffee beverage class; no specific 3-in-1 mocha subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKM covers instant coffee powder; no specific 3-in-1 mocha premix code exists. Used as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for 3-in-1 instant mocha premix beverage.

allergens: milk derivatives (non-dairy creamer may contain casein)

serving.description: 1 sachet (approximately 20 g) dissolved in 150–180 ml hot water
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard sachet of 3-in-1 instant mocha (approximately 20 g) dissolved in 150–180 ml of hot water constitutes a typical single serving.

nutrient_source.title: 3-in-1 Mocha Instant Coffee Beverage — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 3-in-1 mocha instant coffee beverage (cr_id D09010011). Values represent the dry powder per 100 g as reported.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 3-in-1 instant mocha premix powder. GI estimated at 53 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for instant coffee with sugar and non-dairy creamer, which is the closest analogous product. According to the University of Sydney GI Database, a GI of 53 falls into the 'low' category (GI <= 55). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (53) × carbohydrate per serving (0.85 g from 20 g sachet × 4.24 g/100 g) / 100 = 0.45, rounded to 0.5. The low GL reflects the small carbohydrate mass per serving despite the low GI.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar with negligible fibre and potassium; non-dairy creamer likely contains palm-based saturated fat
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is very low (5 mg/100 g), individuals with hypertension should limit this beverage because the added sugar (3.0 g/100 g), negligible dietary fibre (0.28 g/100 g), and low potassium (~40 mg/100 g) provide no compensating cardiovascular benefit, and the non-dairy creamer fraction likely contributes saturated fat that can adversely affect vascular health; plain black coffee or unsweetened cocoa is preferred.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) due to added sugar in a manufactured instant mix; non-dairy creamer may worsen insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: Under ADA guidelines, this 3-in-1 instant mocha is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage regardless of the low absolute sugar quantity (3.0 g/100 g), and individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid it entirely, substituting plain black coffee or unsweetened cocoa to prevent postprandial glucose excursions and insulin resistance worsened by the non-dairy creamer.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from non-dairy creamer (estimated ~1.7 g/100 g) and trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) from potentially hydrogenated vegetable oil in creamer fraction
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The non-dairy creamer in this 3-in-1 mocha likely contains palm-based saturated fat (~1.7 g/100 g) and possible trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) from hydrogenated vegetable oil, both of which raise LDL cholesterol and increase cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this product and choose plain black coffee or cocoa made with low-fat dairy or plant-based milk without hydrogenated oils.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory saturated and trans fat from non-dairy creamer; insufficient polyphenol content from cocoa fraction
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Although cocoa contains polyphenols with potential anti-inflammatory properties, the non-dairy creamer in this 3-in-1 mocha likely contributes palm-based saturated fat and trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), which are pro-inflammatory; the cocoa content in a 3-in-1 premix is insufficient to provide meaningful anti-inflammatory benefit, and individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should choose unsweetened pure cocoa or dark chocolate (>70% cocoa) instead.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 3-in-1 Instant Mocha is an ultra-processed instant beverage premix combining instant coffee, cocoa powder, non-dairy creamer, and sugar in a single sachet, designed to be dissolved in hot water to produce a ready-to-drink mocha-flavoured coffee beverage. It is widely consumed across Singapore and Southeast Asia as a convenient breakfast or snack drink. The beverage has a sweet, mildly bitter chocolate-coffee flavour with a creamy texture imparted by the non-dairy creamer. Nutritionally, the dry powder provides 36 kcal per 100 g, with modest protein (0.55 g), fat (1.92 g, predominantly saturated from palm-based creamer), and carbohydrate (4.24 g, of which 2.98 g is sugar). Dietary fibre is negligible (0.28 g/100 g) and sodium is very low (5 mg/100 g). However, the presence of added sugar classifies this product as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under ADA and WHO guidelines, making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The non-dairy creamer fraction likely contains palm oil-derived saturated fat and potentially hydrogenated vegetable oil contributing trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), which are pro-inflammatory and atherogenic. A standard serving is one sachet (approximately 20 g), yielding approximately 7.2 kcal and 0.85 g carbohydrate per serving, resulting in a low glycemic load despite a low estimated GI of 53. Plain black coffee or unsweetened cocoa are clinically preferred alternatives for health-conscious consumers.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a sachet of 3-in-1 mocha?
Is instant mocha coffee high in sugar?
Can I drink 3-in-1 coffee if I have heart disease?
What is the glycemic index of instant mocha?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09010011, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. The input record is marked as reviewed with canonical provenance. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this 3-in-1 mocha premix is not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry; only total fat is available. 2. saturated_fat_g (1.70 g/100 g) is estimated from the non-dairy creamer fraction composition typical of palm-based creamers used in Southeast Asian 3-in-1 coffee mixes; this is an approximation as the HPB record does not separately report saturated fat. 3. trans_fat_g (0.1 g/100 g) is estimated based on the health_suitability caution in the input record referencing trans fat from potentially hydrogenated vegetable oil in the creamer fraction; this is an approximation. 4. potassium_mg (40 mg/100 g) is estimated from the input health_suitability notes referencing ~40 mg/100 g; this is an approximation as HPB does not separately report potassium for this entry. 5. calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 and iron_mg as 0.0 per the HPB source; these are confirmed zero values as reported. 6. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as non-dairy creamer does not contain cholesterol. 7. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this product. 8. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for instant coffee with sugar and non-dairy creamer; no direct GI measurement for 3-in-1 mocha premix powder was found. According to the University of Sydney GI Database, a GI of 53 falls into the 'low' category (GI <= 55). 9. The dietary_tags 'keto' in the input record is incorrect for this product given the presence of added sugar and carbohydrates; this tag has been removed. 10. The input health_suitability condition 'diabetes' has been mapped to 'type2_diabetes' per Veritas naming rules.
